items.headline: City launching amnesty program for unlicensed businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out a business license amnesty program targeting shopping centers, allowing businesses operating without proper licenses to get compliant without penalties. This could affect how many local shops operate and what gets enforced going forward.

items.headline: New incentives coming for retail and startup businesses
items.summary: The city is introducing economic development incentives specifically designed to attract and support retail shops and startups in Cupertino. This is a concrete move to boost local business activity and commercial diversity.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing development approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the March 24 meeting. This marks a second reading vote, indicating the project is moving forward after initial approval.

items.headline: Mountain View awarded state funding for housing incentive program
items.summary: The city applied for and received state funding through California's Prohousing Incentive Program, Round 4, which supports local zoning and planning changes that increase housing production. This funding can help Mountain View accelerate housing development projects.

items.headline: City seeking public input on $HUD housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting comments on how to spend federal HUD housing grant money for the 2026-27 fiscal year. Residents can weigh in on priorities like affordable housing, homelessness services, or other housing-related needs at the March 24 meeting.

items.headline: Sunnyvale hosting community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: The city held a public meeting on March 23 to discuss its safe parking initiative. This program typically addresses vehicle dwelling and homeless services, suggesting Sunnyvale is expanding or modifying how it handles parking for people living in cars.
